channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Matt Wagner of Danish Maid Butter on the art and tradition of Easter butter lambs
item.description: NPR's Don Gonyea talks with Matt Wagner, co-owner of Danish Maid Butter in Chicago, about the little Easter lambs made of butter that sell around the country this time of year.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Don Gonyea talks with Matt Wagner, co-owner of Danish Maid Butter in Chicago, about the little Easter lambs made of butter that sell around the country this time of year.
item.dc:creator: Don Gonyea

item.title: An exhibit in New York examines master painter Raphael in the U.S. for the first time
item.description: For the first time in the United States, there's a major retrospective of the work of the Renaissance master Raphael.
item.content:encoded: For the first time in the United States, there's a major retrospective of the work of the Renaissance master Raphael.

item.title: Sunday Puzzle: Vowel Renewals
item.description: NPR's Don Gonyea plays the puzzle with KXJZ radio listener Suzanne Palmer and Weekend Edition Puzzlemaster Will Shortz.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Don Gonyea plays the puzzle with KXJZ radio listener Suzanne Palmer and Weekend Edition Puzzlemaster Will Shortz.
item.dc:creator: Will Shortz

item.title: 'Wait Wait' for March 28, 2026: Live in Savannah with D.W. Moffett
item.description: This week, Wait Wait is live in Savannah with host Peter Sagal, guest Judge and Scorekeeper Alzo Slade, special guest D.W. Moffett and panelists Adam Burke, Shantira Jackson, and Joyelle Nicole Johnson
item.content:encoded:
This week, Wait Wait is live in Savannah with host Peter Sagal, guest Judge and Scorekeeper Alzo Slade, special guest D.W. Moffett and panelists Adam Burke, Shantira Jackson, and Joyelle Nicole Johnson

(Image credit: Vivien Killilea)

item.title: 'The Comeback' is back. That's something to Cherish
item.description: The third (and final) installment of this Hollywood satire finds C-lister Valerie Cherish (Lisa Kudrow) helming an AI-written show.
item.content:encoded:
The third (and final) installment of this Hollywood satire finds C-lister Valerie Cherish (Lisa Kudrow) helming an AI-written show.

(Image credit: Erin Simkin)
item.dc:creator: Glen Weldon

item.title: That was a fun movie. But where are the bloopers nowadays?
item.description: Bloopers have usually been funny endnotes to funny movies. They peaked in the late 1990s and early 2000s, but are seemingly fading away.
item.content:encoded:
Bloopers have usually been funny endnotes to funny movies. They peaked in the late 1990s and early 2000s, but are seemingly fading away.

(Image credit: Evan Agostini)
item.dc:creator: Ayana Archie

item.title: The iconic South African theater that took on apartheid
item.description: South Africa's iconic Market Theatre, born in the darkest days of apartheid and a force for change, is celebrating its 50th anniversary.
item.content:encoded:
South Africa's iconic Market Theatre, born in the darkest days of apartheid and a force for change, is celebrating its 50th anniversary.

(Image credit: Ruphin Coudyzer)
item.dc:creator: Kate Bartlett

item.title: Jigsaw puzzling is a growing competitive activity — with a national tournament
item.description: There's a big national competition in Atlanta this week in puzzling. Teams will race to put jigsaw pieces into place, assembling 500 and 1,000 piece puzzles they've never seen.
item.content:encoded: There's a big national competition in Atlanta this week in puzzling. Teams will race to put jigsaw pieces into place, assembling 500 and 1,000 piece puzzles they've never seen.
item.dc:creator: Leslie E. Thompson

item.title: 'The Madison' adds to Taylor Sheridan's 'Yellowstone' legacy — 'Marshals' not so much
item.description: Yellowstone's creator is back with two new shows set in the American West. Marshals struggles, but The Madison offers a thoughtful portrait of a family in flux.
item.content:encoded:
Yellowstone's creator is back with two new shows set in the American West. Marshals struggles, but The Madison offers a thoughtful portrait of a family in flux.

(Image credit: Emerson Miller)
item.dc:creator: David Bianculli

item.title: Strangers forge a powerful bond after a car crash in 'Miroirs No. 3'
item.description: After the sudden death of her boyfriend, a young Berlin woman is taken in by a family she meets in the countryside. In showing the ache of love and loss, Miroirs No. 3 holds up a mirror to us all.
item.content:encoded: After the sudden death of her boyfriend, a young Berlin woman is taken in by a family she meets in the countryside. In showing the ache of love and loss, Miroirs No. 3 holds up a mirror to us all.
item.dc:creator: Justin Chang
